title: Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum
intro: Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea form to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum is a species of orchid in the genus Cleisostoma. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The species falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae, a group of orchids that share similar characteristics. Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum further classified as tribe Coelogyneae, which consists of several species of orchids that share similar features. At the highest taxonomic rank, Cleisostoma fuscomaculatum is classified as a flowering plant in the kingdom Plantae.
